title: Bobua montana
intro:

However, I found information on the plant "Bobua" (which is also known as "Buddleja montana" or "Bobua montana" in some sources, but most widely known as "Buddleja montana") and I will provide the information you requested based on that.

Buddleja montana, also known as Mountain Butterfly Bush or Mountain Buddleja, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of northern India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and fever, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Buddleja montana include flavonoids, such as flavone glycosides and flavanone glycosides, as well as phenolic acids and sesquiterpenes. The most common herbal preparations made with Buddleja montana include decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.

uses: Bobua montana, a plant species found in the tropical regions of Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The leaves and roots of this plant are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its anti-inflammatory properties are also believed to help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Furthermore, the plant is thought to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm the medicinal uses of Bobua montana.
properties: Bobua montana has been reported to have various therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
